name: Marfan Syndrome
summary: Marfan syndrome is a hereditary connective tissue disorder most commonly caused by pathogenic FBN1 variants. GENARCH provides educational summaries only.
adolescent_relevance: Often identified in childhood/adolescence; included as monogenic architecture example (educational only).

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS not applicable as primary model for monogenic Marfan syndrome.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: General cardiovascular physiology context; not treated as medical advice.
General cardiovascular physiology context; not treated as medical advice.
Systemic stressor context; evidence specific to Marfan is limited.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: Variant catalogs and clinical research have uneven representation across populations.
population_equity.transferability_notes: Variant interpretation can be impacted by underrepresentation in reference datasets.

references.title: Marfan syndrome: FBN1, connective tissue, and clinical features (review)
WHO guidelines on physical activity and sedentary behaviour
Psychosocial stress and health outcomes: mechanisms and evidence (review)
Air pollution and health: evidence and mechanisms (review)
references.journal: Journal of the Air & Waste Management Association
